title: Assessing the Generalizability of Mobile Software Engineering Research Through Combined Systematic Methods
abstract: Mobile Software Engineering has emerged as a distinct subfield, raising questions about the transferability of its research findings to general software engineering. This paper addresses the challenge of evaluating the generalizability of mobile-specific research, using Green Computing as a representative case.

title: Extensibility, Model Interpretability and Explainability, and Automation in ML.NET: A Comprehensive Analysis
The Past, Present, and Future of Mobile Software Engineering
Highly Interpretable Prediction Models for SNP Data
venue: Proceedings of the 18th International Joint Conference on Biomedical Engineering Systems and Technologies, Volume 1: BIOINFORMATICS

title: logicGP - A Framework for Literal Based Classification with a Focus on Software Architecture and Open Source Implementation
venue: Proceedings of the Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference Companion
abstract: This paper presents a Genetic Programming based framework called logicGP for classification with literal based models and the open source software created and used for the implementation. The implementation is done in C# and is designed for high compatibility with Microsoft's ML.NET.

title: Possibilities and Limitations of Mobile Applications for Controlling
venue: The Digitalization of Management Accounting: Use Cases from Theory and Practice

title: Using Hexagonal Architecture for Mobile Applications
venue: Proceedings of the 17th International Conference on Software Technologies - ICSOFT

title: Choosing a Global Architecture for Mobile Applications
abstract: Complex mobile applications require an appropriate global architecture. This paper gives an overview of the current situation and architectural choices and proposes a mix-and-match approach suitable for both small and large projects.

title: Beyond Web/Native/Hybrid: A New Taxonomy for Mobile App Development
venue: Proceedings of the 5th International Conference on Mobile Software Engineering and Systems
abstract: A new taxonomy for multi-platform mobile application development is proposed, distinguishing six categories based on tools and programming languages rather than the older web/native/hybrid shorthand.

title: Methods for Identifying SNP Interactions: A Review on Variations of Logic Regression, Random Forest and Bayesian Logistic Regression
authors: Carla C.M. Chen, Holger Schwender, Jonthan Keith, Robin Nunkesser, Kerrie Mengersen and Paula Macrossan
venue: IEEE/ACM Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics

title: Analysis of a genetic programming algorithm for association studies
venue: GECCO '08: Proceedings of the 10th Annual Conference on Genetic and Evolutionary Computation

title: Online analysis of time series by the Qn estimator
authors: Robin Nunkesser, Roland Fried, Karen Schettlinger and Ursula Gather
abstract: A fast update algorithm for online calculation of the Qn scale estimator enables robust real-time analysis of high-frequency time series even in the presence of large outliers.

title: RFreak - An R Package for Evolutionary Computation
Applying the Qn Estimator Online
authors: Robin Nunkesser, Karen Schettlinger and Roland Fried
venue: Data Analysis, Machine Learning and Applications

title: Detecting High-Order Interactions of Single Nucleotide Polymorphisms Using Genetic Programming
authors: Robin Nunkesser, Thorsten Bernholt, Holger Schwender, Katja Ickstadt and Ingo Wegener
abstract: This article presents a procedure based on genetic programming and multi-valued logic that enables the identification of high-order interactions of categorical variables such as SNPs and applies it to breast cancer and HapMap data.
